rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GONZALEZ VS. DWAIN ALAN DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday March 27, 2015, Line 1, Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to lift stay is granted. The Court initially referred the motion to Hon. Harold Kahn whose Order Staying Discovery dated March 9, 2012 is requested to be lifted. Judge Kahn declined to hear the motion and referred it back to Dept. 302. On the merits the motion is granted based on the changed circumstance the action is set for trial by order on August 24, 2015. In addition the action was filed on October 21, 2010 and the mandate of Code of Civil Procedure Section 583.310 is approaching. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARL HILSZ VS VERA CORT, et a
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Award Of Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 27, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT VERA CORT Motion For An Award Of Attorney. The motion is granted in its entirety. In State ex rel. Standard Elevator Co. v. West Bay Builders, Inc. (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 963, 982, the court provides that: A claim is clearly frivolous if it is " 'utterly lacking in legal merit and evidentiary support.' " [citation] Stated otherwise, a claim is clearly frivolous "when, viewed objectively, it may be said to have no reasonable chance of success, and present no valid argument to modify present law." In this case, the record shows that this action was frivolous. The contentious history between the parties shows that the purpose of the complaint was to harass defendant. The action lacked merit from the start because CCSF was already auditing defendant. Lastly, the record shows that plaintiff did little to no investigation to support his allegations. Plaintiff shall pay to defendant attorney's fees in the amount of $126,882.50 and expenses in the amount of $2,161.54. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KUNDRY RIVERO MUJICA ET AL VS. ISMAEL BASCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 27, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT ISMAEL BASCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. As to the first, second, and seventh Cause of Action the demurrer is overruled. The Complaint adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the cause of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action: Violation of Civil Code Section 1708.8. Each Cause of Action within the Complaint must properly identify Defendants [See California Rule of Court 2.112]. rulings.Case Title: JANET KASSOUF VS. RICHARD WEAVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Law Offices Of John B. Mcmorrow
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 27, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF Motion To Disqualify Law Offices Of John B. McMorrow. The court denies the motion to disqualify as moot. The motion sought to disqualify Mr. McMorrow and he rendered the motion moot by substituting out of the case. While there may be a problem with the corporation's new counsel, the moving papers do not sufficiently brief that issue. Plaintiff cannot bring an entirely new motion in the reply brief and the corporation's new counsel should have an opportunity to respond. Plaintiff needs to file a new motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
